water_temp_read.read: Water temperature is in a favorable trout range.

light_pressure_read.read: High pressure and low cloud cover make this fishable but technical.

stocking_read.read: Recent stocking supports eggs, worms, and attractor nymphs.

angler_analysis.expected_trout_behavior: Expect trout to favor the most comfortable feeding lanes while staying close to cover when light is high.
angler_analysis.presentation_style: Prioritize clean drifts, smaller natural patterns, and stocked-trout attractors where appropriate.

fly_reasoning.egg_pattern: Recent stocking makes eggs a logical confidence fly.
fly_reasoning.squirmy_worm: A simple stocked-trout attractor and good dirty-water option.
fly_reasoning.duracell: A strong attractor nymph when fish will move a little but are not fully aggressive.
fly_reasoning.zebra_midge: Small and natural for clear, pressured, technical conditions.
fly_reasoning.pheasant_tail: A reliable natural nymph when trout are feeding but cautious.

caution_notes: Bright/high-pressure conditions may make fish cover-oriented and less forgiving.

generated_text.quick_read: The Chattahoochee River is offering good but technical trout fishing conditions with ideal water temperatures and recent stocking support. Expect trout to be selective and favor shaded and structured areas due to bright, high pressure.
generated_text.best_window: The best window is day 1 morning due to lower wind, moderate sky cover, and minimal rain probability creating ideal conditions for trout to feed along riffles, runs, seams, and structure with smaller natural flies and careful presentations.
generated_text.current_conditions: Current USGS conditions show a water temperature of 58.6°F, flow at 768 cfs, gage height 1.82 ft, and low turbidity at 2.4 NTU, indicating clear and favorable water for trout activity.
generated_text.weather_setup: Weather features mostly clear skies with 22% average cloud cover, stable high pressure at 30.1 inHg, light northeast wind averaging 1.6 mph, and only a 16% chance of rain, supporting technical but good fishing.
generated_text.what_this_means: Trout are likely to stay close to cover in deeper runs, undercut banks, and shaded areas to avoid bright conditions. Presentations should prioritize clean drifts and smaller natural or stocked trout attractor flies like egg patterns, squirmy worms, and duracells. Use nymphs, midges, and cautious streamer approaches. Exercise caution as bright, high-pressure conditions can make trout less forgiving and more cover-oriented.
